title: Retrieval Review: Overflow, Geometry, and Effects
summary: Retrieval Review: Overflow, Geometry, and Effects retrieves prior skills in an original changed content paint and context constraints scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css overflow, css sizing constraints, css transforms, css filters, css shadows depth, css containment effects, css effects debugging in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed content paint and context constraints artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

options.text: Changed content paint and context constraints case 1: overflow hidden is a harmless way to repair any layout that extends beyond a card.

Changed content paint and context constraints case 1: identify the overflowing content and sizing cause, preserve access, and use auto, scroll, clip, hidden, or visible only for an intentional contract.
Changed content paint and context constraints case 1: apply overflow hidden to the body so no component can cause horizontal scrolling.

options.text: Changed content paint and context constraints case 5: more shadows always make interactive elevation clearer.
Changed content paint and context constraints case 5: use restrained shadows as secondary presentation and keep boundaries, hierarchy, and focus understandable with shadows removed.

options.text: Changed content paint and context constraints case 11: a brightness filter reliably repairs contrast for arbitrary images and text.

Changed content paint and context constraints case 11: use filters only for bounded visual treatment, preserve an unfiltered fallback, and remeasure contrast, legibility, and performance on actual content.

options.text: Changed content paint and context constraints case 16: fixed height is the most reliable way to keep cards aligned.
Changed content paint and context constraints case 16: use intrinsic sizing and min or max constraints, allow content-driven block growth, and test extreme content before adding fixed dimensions.
Changed content paint and context constraints case 16: use max-content for every narrow-screen control because intrinsic width always fits the viewport.
